steps.title: Use the Amur grape values
steps.content: The example must support the statement that opposite-side orientation was much more common than same-side orientation. For the Amur grape, the graph shows a much taller bar for opposite-side orientation than for same-side orientation.

steps.title: Keep the counts accurate
steps.content: Choice B gives the wrong same-side count, Choice C incorrectly says no pairs showed same-side orientation, and Choice D reverses the two values. The remaining choice matches the graph: about 150 opposite-side pairs and about 60 same-side pairs. The answer is A.
